fields.value: Fig. 1 data: yield stress increases with Al to a maximum at 25 at.% Al, then falls rapidly at 25-28% Al, then slight increase or little change to 40% Al; ductility drops with increasing Al, with slight maximum at 28% Al. (Text and figure caption).

support.reason: Relevant context was recalled, but the exact field value was not confirmed.

fields.value: Fig. 2 data: work hardening rate (secant over 1-1.5% strain) is low for <20% Al, sharp peak at 28-29% Al, moderate above 35% Al; addition of 0.1% B has little consistent effect. (Text and figure caption).

support.reason: Relevant context was recalled, but the exact field value was not confirmed.

fields.value: Fig. 3 data: APB energy variations with Al content; APBI and APBII energies from Refs. [19-21]. (Figure caption).

support.reason: Relevant context was recalled, but the exact field value was not confirmed.

fields.value: Fig. 4 data: elastic stiffness constants C11, C12, C44 and Zener anisotropy factor A = 2C44/(C11-C12) vary with Al content; high anisotropy near Fe3Al composition. (Figure caption).

support.reason: Relevant context was recalled, but the exact field value was not confirmed.

fields.value: The peak work hardening at 28% Al is associated with increased elastic anisotropy and strong Peierls forces favoring screw dislocation orientation; also, sessile <100> edge dislocations from reactions contribute to hardening.

support.reason: Relevant context was recalled, but the exact field value was not confirmed.

fields.value: No APB tubes were observed; hence the APB tube model for work hardening receives little support in this study.

support.reason: Relevant context was recalled, but the exact field value was not confirmed.

fields.value: Considere criterion: the small ductility peak at 28% Al is related to high work hardening rate over the composition range where flow stress is relatively constant.

support.reason: Relevant context was recalled, but the exact field value was not confirmed.
